neighborhoods.name: Cotacachi Centro
neighborhoods.description: Small indigenous-mestizo town known for leather goods, with a central park and surrounding volcanoes
neighborhoods.character: Small Andean town, leather craft culture, indigenous markets

housing.predominantType: Colonial houses and small apartments near the park

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Cotacachi. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: La Quinta Area
neighborhoods.description: Rural area between Cotacachi and Lake Cuicocha with farming communities and mountain views
neighborhoods.character: Rural Andean, farming, volcano and lake views

housing.predominantType: Houses on agricultural land and rural properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Cotacachi's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Quiroga
neighborhoods.description: Adjacent village with a weekly indigenous market and very affordable housing
neighborhoods.character: Indigenous village, very affordable, traditional market
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Los Portales / Upper Cotacachi
neighborhoods.description: Hillside area above town where many North American expats have settled with organized community activities
neighborhoods.character: Expat hillside, community groups, mountain views
neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
